journal_entry.reason: Acquisition of FVTPL financial asset; transaction costs expensed per IFRS 9 for FVTPL classification. Investment recorded at purchase price; fee paid separately reduces cash.

output.asset_increase_reason: Financial asset at FVTPL (Ford shares) increases by $3,100,000. The $3,000,000 acquisition cost and $100,000 transaction fee are both capitalized to the investment under IFRS 9 initial recognition at fair value plus transaction costs directly attributable to the acquisition. This is one asset account (Investment in equity securities - FVTPL), so one line.
output.dividend_increase_reason: No owner withdrawals or dividends declared/distributed in this transaction.
output.expense_increase_reason: The $100,000 transaction fee is capitalized to the investment asset, not expensed, because it is directly attributable to the acquisition of the financial asset at initial recognition under IFRS 9.
output.liability_decrease_reason: No liabilities are being paid down or extinguished in this transaction.
output.equity_decrease_reason: No equity accounts are reduced in this transaction.
output.revenue_decrease_reason: No revenue accounts are reduced in this transaction.

output.liability_increase_reason: No liabilities are created or increased. The transaction involves an outright purchase of shares paid by cheque (cash), not financed through borrowing or credit.
output.equity_increase_reason: No equity accounts increase. BlackRock is the acquirer purchasing shares as an investment asset, not issuing its own equity or receiving capital contributions.
output.revenue_increase_reason: No revenue is earned. This is an investment acquisition transaction, not a sale of goods or services or earning of income.
output.asset_decrease_reason: Cash decreases for the total payment of $3,100,000 ($3,000,000 for shares + $100,000 transaction fee) paid by cheque. This is a single cash outflow, counted as one line even though it covers two economic components, because both use the same account (Cash/Bank) with the same treatment (decrease). The transaction fee is capitalized as part of the initial cost of the FVTPL financial asset under IFRS 9, so both amounts reduce cash in a single credit entry.
output.dividend_decrease_reason: No dividends or owner drawings are reversed or decreased. This is an investment purchase transaction.
output.expense_decrease_reason: No expenses are reversed or decreased. The transaction fee is capitalized as part of the cost of the FVTPL investment (included in the initial measurement), not expensed, so there is no expense decrease.

output_tax_specialist.reasoning: Transaction involves acquisition of equity shares (financial asset) and payment of transaction fees. Equity investments and financial asset purchases are not taxable supplies. Transaction fees related to financial instruments are also not subject to tax. No tax is mentioned in the transaction text.
